title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics are now a favorite in the East after unexpectedly thriving without Jayson Tatum.
Steve Kerr praised the Celtics for developing younger players during Tatum's absence, enhancing team depth.
Boston leads in offensive efficiency with strong spacing, contributing to their dominance over the Warriors.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester for sending armed police to all mental health 911 calls.
The lawsuit highlights incidents where police responses allegedly escalated situations involving mental health crises.
Less than 25% of Worcester police completed crisis intervention training, despite reports of civil rights violations.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff will speak at Northeastern University's 2026 commencement in Fenway Park on April 29.
Duff, known for 'Lizzy Maguire' and 'A Cinderella Story,' was announced in a Y2K-themed social media post.
Past Northeastern commencement speakers include David A. Thomas, Barbara Pierce Bush, and John F. Kennedy.

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a No.1 wide receiver, considering A.J. Brown as a trade target ahead of the 2026 NFL season.
New England aims to bolster their pass rush, eyeing potential draft picks like Texas A&M's Cashius Howell.
Patriots prioritize offensive tackle depth, potentially targeting Clemson's Blake Miller in the 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham was charged with murder in the October shooting of Marie Molea-Anthony in Roxbury.
The teen, already in custody on unrelated charges, faces counts including murder, aggravated assault, and firearm possession.
Boston police continue to investigate and urge anyone with information to contact them or use the CrimeStoppers tip line.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., 64, was released after spending 34 years in prison for a 1985 murder conviction.
Suffolk County District Attorney dropped charges citing new forensic evidence, lost evidence, and changes in case law.
Rosa becomes the 100th exoneree from Massachusetts since 1989, supported by the New England Innocence Project.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, was sentenced to 35-40 years in prison for raping a 5-year-old girl in 2022.
Machado was convicted on multiple charges including child rape and indecent assault in Fall River Superior Court.
Machado moved to Chicago after the 2022 incident and was later arrested with the help of US Marshals.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley hockey team won their first R.I. Division II state championship, defeating Lincoln High School 3-2 in quadruple overtime.
Colin Dorgan's late regulation goal and recent successes carried BVS through, following a personal tragedy impacting his family.
The team wore a heart emblem with initials of Dorgan's deceased family members, motivating their playoff performance.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins recalled forward Lukas Reichel from Providence Bruins on an emergency basis ahead of Thursday's game.
Reichel, a 2020 first-round pick, was acquired from the Canucks in exchange for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
In three games with Providence, Reichel scored one goal and four assists, showing potential in boosting Bruins' offense.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough town leaders approved a license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, ending relocation concerns.
Boston Soccer 2026 and Kraft Sports & Entertainment agreed on a funding deal for estimated $7.8 million in security costs.
The license includes a traffic management plan and enhanced MBTA service, accommodating 21,000 patrons per game.
